name: Palau
avoid: June–October is wetter and more humid, so visibility, jellyfish-lake conditions, and boat schedules are less predictable than the November–May dry season.
Rock Islands permits, Jellyfish Lake access, and Blue Corner dive boats add up quickly, so Palau is rarely a bargain destination even on a short trip.
Jellyfish Lake openings and jellyfish numbers can fluctuate with climate cycles, so confirm current access before locking your day-by-day plan.
stopoverNote: For trips under 5 days, base yourself in Koror and keep Peleliu as a single long day or skip it; once you add multiple boat days, Palau feels expensive and transfer-heavy very quickly.

cities.name: Koror
cities.notes: Koror is Palau's practical base for museums, marinas, dive shops, hotel choices, and taxi-linked access to nearly every shared boat trip into the Rock Islands.

cities.name: Rock Islands (Jellyfish Lake)
cities.notes: The Rock Islands are Palau's signature limestone-lagoon playground for Jellyfish Lake, Long Beach, the Milky Way, German Channel, and the boat-heavy snorkeling days that justify the trip.

cities.name: Peleliu
cities.notes: Peleliu adds the most important WWII battlefield landscapes in Palau, plus a quieter island stay if you want one more night beyond Koror's marina-and-tour rhythm.

itinerary.sim: PalauCel is the simplest prepaid choice around Koror and the main tourism corridor, while PNCC is the usual backup; download offline maps and operator vouchers before you leave the marina because boat days can go off-grid fast.

Koror.name: Koror
Koror.note: Use Koror as the sleep-easy base for taxis, marina departures, museum time, and your big Blue Corner or Rock Islands days.

days.theme: Museum Stops, Marina Bearings and a Gentle Koror Landing

activities.name: Belau National Museum
activities.tip: Adult non-resident admission is US$15 and is the best first stop for Palauan history before you spend money on permits and reef days.

activities.name: Etpison Museum
activities.tip: General admission is US$10, making it an easy second museum for contemporary Palauan art, storytelling, and carved cultural objects.

activities.name: Taxi transfer around Koror and Malakal marinas
activities.tip: Koror taxis are not metered; short in-town hops are usually negotiated, while airport transfers often land closer to ₹1,660–₹2,490 per vehicle.

activities.name: Ngermalk Trail or Malakal waterfront sunset

days.theme: Blue Corner Diving or an Easy Marina Reset Day

activities.name: Blue Corner 2-tank dive with Blue Marlin or a similar Koror dive operator
activities.tip: A typical 2-tank outer-reef dive runs about US$176 before permits; add the Rock Islands permit unless you already hold the Jellyfish Lake permit.

activities.name: Koror State Rock Islands permit
activities.tip: The standard 5-day permit is US$50 and covers diving, snorkeling, and sightseeing in the Rock Islands Southern Lagoon, but not Jellyfish Lake.

activities.name: Dolphins Pacific visit or marina-side coffee break

Rock Islands (Jellyfish Lake).name: Rock Islands (Jellyfish Lake)
Rock Islands (Jellyfish Lake).note: Give the Rock Islands two full days if you can: one permit-heavy Jellyfish Lake day and one slower Long Beach or German Channel snorkel loop.

days.theme: Jellyfish Lake, Milky Way and the Classic Permit-Heavy First Boat Day

activities.name: Koror State Jellyfish Lake permit
activities.tip: The 5-day Jellyfish Lake permit is US$100 and already includes Rock Islands access, so do not buy the US$50 Rock Islands permit separately if the lake is on your plan.

activities.name: Shared Rock Islands boat tour covering Jellyfish Lake and Milky Way
activities.tip: Most full-day shared trips from Koror are about US$115–150 excluding permits, usually with lunch, hotel pickup, and snorkel gear.

activities.name: Jellyfish Lake snorkel
activities.tip: Check current lake health before booking because access or jellyfish density can change after warmer climate cycles.

days.theme: Long Beach, German Channel and a Slower Snorkeling Day

activities.name: Long Beach and Rock Islands full-day snorkeling tour
activities.tip: Shared full-day Long Beach tours typically run US$100–125 before permits and usually include lunch, gear, and Koror hotel pickup.

activities.name: Kayak or SUP add-on in sheltered Rock Islands lagoons
activities.tip: Guided kayak-and-snorkel outings often price near US$114–118 with equipment and transfers bundled.

Peleliu.name: Peleliu
Peleliu.note: Peleliu is best as a focused WWII-history add-on: either a long guided day trip from Koror or one quiet overnight that lets the battlefield stops breathe.

days.theme: Battlefield History, Orange Beach and a Quiet Island Night

activities.name: Peleliu Island Historical Tour
activities.tip: Full-day guided tours from Koror usually price around US$146–154 and commonly include round-trip boat transfer, land transport, lunch, museum access, and the Peleliu permit.

activities.name: Peleliu WWII Museum and Orange Beach independent stop
activities.tip: If you arrange your own transport, budget about US$15–20 for the Peleliu land permit, with museum access often included or only a very small extra fee.

activities.name: Storyboard reef-edge swim or sunset walk

connections.method: Pre-booked hotel shuttle or a negotiated taxi into central Koror or Malakal; taxis are the default because Palau has no broad public-airport bus network.
Shared speedboat day tour from a Koror or Malakal marina; boats are essential because there is no land access into the lagoon system.
Public ferry or passenger boat for the slower budget route, or a PMA scheduled domestic flight / air taxi if you want to skip the long sea crossing.
Most travelers route back through Koror before continuing south; direct charters are possible but shared-boat logistics are far simpler from the main marinas.
Return by public boat/ferry or by PMA's scheduled service when seats are available; flights are much faster but boats remain the normal traveler choice.
PMA domestic flights or air taxis connect Koror with places such as Angaur and occasionally Peleliu when schedules line up; confirm availability directly before planning onward stays.

extras: Palau uses US dollars on the ground, so most operators quote permits and tours in USD first; card acceptance is decent in Koror hotels and dive shops, but keep cash for ferries, taxis, and small island purchases.
Boats are the backbone of a Palau trip: even world-famous stops such as Jellyfish Lake, Long Beach, German Channel, and Blue Corner depend on sea conditions and operator routing each day.
Koror taxis are negotiated rather than metered, so settle the fare before you get in—especially for airport runs, late dinners on Malakal, or dawn departures to the marina.

If you want to extend beyond the classic Koror-Rock Islands-Peleliu loop, PMA flights to outlying states can save a lot of time, but schedules are thin enough that you should treat them as pre-booked logistics, not spontaneous hops.
